title: Network Security, Attacks, Segmentation, and Hardening · Operations Brief: Net Iam Authentication
summary: Threat-model and harden enterprise, guest, IoT, OT, management, and remote-access network zones. Planned artifact: Add and verify the net iam authentication increment in a defense-in-depth network security plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use MFA, SSO, RADIUS, LDAP, SAML, TACACS+, time-based authentication, authorization, RBAC, and least privilege appropriately.
Protect data in transit and at rest and explain certificates, PKI trust, self-signed limits, key management, and rotation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
